title: Community Composting Program
text:
The City of Riverside recently launched a mandatory composting program for all residential properties. Starting next month, households will be required to separate organic waste — such as food scraps and yard trimmings — into a designated green bin provided by the city.

The program is part of a broader strategy to divert organic waste from landfills, which currently account for nearly 40% of the city's total waste output. City officials estimate that the initiative could reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 25% over the next decade.

Residents who fail to comply with the new rules may receive a warning for the first offence, followed by a fine of up to $150 for repeated violations. The city will offer free educational workshops throughout the month of June to help residents understand what materials can and cannot be composted.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the composting program?
opts: To increase property taxes
To reduce organic waste sent to landfills
To encourage residents to grow their own food
To replace the current recycling program

questions.q: According to the passage, what happens to residents who repeatedly break the rules?
opts: They are asked to leave the city
Their bins are removed
They receive a fine of up to $150
They must attend a mandatory workshop

questions.q: The word 'divert' in the second paragraph is closest in meaning to:
opts: increase
redirect
destroy
monitor

questions.q: Based on the passage, what can be inferred about residents who do not attend the city's composting workshops?
opts: They will automatically receive a fine
They may find it harder to know which materials are compostable
They will be exempt from the new composting rules
They will receive printed guides in the mail instead

title: Remote Work Policy Update
text:
Following a two-year pilot program, Greenfield Solutions has announced a permanent hybrid work policy for all full-time employees. Under the new policy, staff are required to work from the office a minimum of two days per week, while retaining the flexibility to work remotely for the remaining days.

The decision was based on an internal survey in which 78% of employees reported higher job satisfaction under hybrid arrangements compared to fully in-office work. However, managers expressed concern that fully remote teams showed lower levels of collaboration and mentorship.

The company will provide a monthly stipend of $75 to help employees cover home office expenses such as internet and ergonomic equipment. Employees wishing to work remotely more than three days per week must submit a formal request to their department head for approval.

questions.q: What did the internal survey find?
opts: Most employees preferred fully remote work
Hybrid work increased job satisfaction for most employees
Managers preferred the fully remote model
The pilot program was considered unsuccessful

questions.q: What is the purpose of the $75 monthly stipend?
opts: To reward high-performing employees
To cover commuting costs
To help pay for home office expenses
To fund team social events

questions.q: The word 'stipend' in the final paragraph is closest in meaning to:
opts: penalty
allowance
contract
requirement

questions.q: What does the passage suggest about managers' views on fully remote work?
opts: They strongly support it because it reduces office costs
They are concerned it weakens teamwork and employee development
They believe it increases productivity across all departments
They think it should replace the hybrid model entirely

title: City Transit Expansion
text:
The Regional Transit Authority has approved a $2.4 billion plan to expand the city's rapid transit network over the next eight years. The project includes the construction of two new subway lines, 24 additional stations, and 15 kilometres of new elevated track.

Planners anticipate that the expansion will reduce average commute times by up to 18 minutes for residents in the city's eastern and northern neighbourhoods, which are currently underserved by public transportation.

Construction is set to begin in the spring and will be carried out in phases to minimize disruption to existing services. Residents near construction zones will receive advance notice and can access detailed project timelines through the authority's website. Some road closures and detours should be expected throughout the construction period.

questions.q: Who is expected to benefit most from the transit expansion?
opts: Residents in the city centre
Residents in eastern and northern neighbourhoods
Tourists visiting the city
Commercial truck drivers

questions.q: How will the construction be organized to reduce disruption?
opts: All construction will happen overnight
Work will only take place on weekends
Construction will be carried out in phases
Existing lines will be shut down temporarily

questions.q: The word 'underserved' suggests that these neighbourhoods:
opts: have too many transit options
are located far from the city
do not receive adequate public services
were recently built

questions.q: What can be inferred about residents living near construction zones?
opts: They will be asked to temporarily relocate during construction
They will be kept informed and should expect some road disruptions
They will receive financial compensation for the inconvenience
They will have access to free transit passes during the construction period

title: Tenant Protection Act
text:
The provincial government has introduced new legislation to strengthen tenant protections across the region. Under the Tenant Protection Act, landlords will be required to provide a minimum of 90 days' written notice before a rent increase takes effect. The allowable annual rent increase will also be capped at 2.5%, regardless of market conditions.

The Act also introduces stronger protections against eviction. Landlords who wish to reclaim a unit for personal use must provide supporting documentation and a minimum of six months' notice. Tenants who believe they have been wrongfully evicted can now apply to a newly established Rental Dispute Tribunal.

Housing advocates have praised the legislation as a significant step forward, though some landlord associations have raised concerns that the restrictions may discourage investment in rental housing construction.

questions.q: Under the new Act, how much notice must landlords give before a rent increase?
opts: 30 days
60 days
90 days
6 months

questions.q: What can tenants do if they believe they were wrongfully evicted?
opts: Contact the provincial police
Apply to the Rental Dispute Tribunal
Refuse to leave the property
File a complaint with the landlord association

questions.q: The word 'cap' as used in the passage means to:
opts: increase gradually
set a maximum limit on
calculate the average of
remove entirely

questions.q: Based on the passage, which group is LEAST likely to support the Tenant Protection Act?
opts: Renters who have recently received an eviction notice
Housing advocates working with low-income tenants
Landlords who plan to build new rental units
Tenants currently involved in a dispute with their landlord

title: Library Digital Services
text:
The Westdale Public Library has expanded its digital services to meet growing demand from residents who prefer accessing resources from home. Members can now borrow up to 10 e-books and 5 audiobooks simultaneously through the library's app, with loan periods of three weeks.

In addition to reading materials, the library has launched an online learning platform that provides free access to over 2,000 courses in topics ranging from language learning and coding to business and personal finance. The platform is available to all library card holders at no additional cost.

Library staff note that while digital services have grown significantly, physical attendance at branches has also remained steady. Many residents continue to use the library for in-person study space, community programs, and access to computers and printing services for those without home devices.

questions.q: How many e-books can library members borrow at one time?
What is the online learning platform primarily designed to do?
opts: Replace physical library visits
Provide free educational courses to members
Sell digital textbooks at a discount
Train library staff

questions.q: According to the passage, which statement about physical library attendance is TRUE?
opts: It has declined significantly since digital services expanded
It has remained stable despite the growth of digital services
It has completely replaced digital service use
It is only used by people without internet access

questions.q: What does the passage suggest about library members who do not have computers or internet at home?
opts: They are not eligible to borrow digital resources
They can still access library services by visiting in person
They will receive a free device from the library
They must pay an additional fee to use library computers

title: Workplace Safety Regulations
text:
The provincial government has updated its workplace safety regulations, introducing stricter requirements for employers in high-risk industries such as construction, manufacturing, and healthcare. Under the new rules, all employers must conduct a formal hazard assessment at least once per year and submit a written report to their regional safety authority.

Employers are also now required to provide safety training to new employees within the first week of employment. Previously, the requirement was within the first 30 days. Workers who identify unsafe conditions have the right to refuse work without loss of pay until the issue is resolved.

Inspectors will conduct unannounced visits to workplaces to check compliance, and companies that fail to meet standards may face fines of up to $50,000. Repeat offenders could face temporary suspension of their operating licence.

questions.q: How often must employers now conduct a formal hazard assessment?
opts: Every six months
Once per year
Every two years
Only when a new employee is hired

questions.q: Under the new rules, when must safety training be provided to new employees?
opts: Before the job offer is made
Within the first week
Within the first 30 days
After the probationary period

questions.q: The word 'unannounced' in the final paragraph means the inspections will be:
opts: conducted at night
carried out without prior notice
performed by volunteers
limited to large companies

questions.q: What can be inferred about a company that has already been fined once for non-compliance?
opts: It will be required to retrain all of its employees immediately
It faces the risk of having its operating licence suspended if violations continue
It will no longer be subject to unannounced inspections
It must submit monthly safety reports to the government

title: Urban Green Space Initiative
text:
A new report released by the City Planning Department recommends transforming at least 15% of the city's paved surfaces into green space over the next 20 years. The proposal includes converting underused parking lots, widening sidewalks with tree canopies, and creating small neighbourhood parks in densely built areas.

Research cited in the report indicates that access to green space is associated with reduced stress levels, lower rates of chronic disease, and stronger social cohesion in communities. The report also highlights environmental benefits, including reduced urban heat, improved air quality, and better management of stormwater runoff.

The initiative is expected to cost approximately $180 million over the 20-year period. City staff have proposed funding the project through a combination of development charges, federal grants, and an annual contribution from the city's capital budget.

questions.q: What is the city's target for converting paved surfaces to green space?
opts: 5% over 10 years
10% over 15 years
15% over 20 years
20% over 25 years

questions.q: According to the report, which is NOT mentioned as a benefit of green space?
opts: Reduced stress levels
Lower property taxes
Improved air quality
Better stormwater management

questions.q: How does the city plan to fund the initiative?
opts: Entirely through federal grants
Only through property tax increases
Through development charges, federal grants, and capital budget contributions
By selling unused city-owned land

questions.q: Based on the report, which type of area would benefit most from the green space initiative?
opts: Suburban neighbourhoods with large private gardens
Densely built urban areas with limited existing green space
Rural communities on the city's outskirts
Industrial zones near the city's waterfront

title: Consumer Protection Update
text:
New consumer protection regulations that came into effect this month give buyers greater rights when purchasing goods and services online. Under the updated rules, online retailers must clearly display total costs — including all taxes, fees, and shipping charges — before a customer confirms a purchase. Hidden fees added after checkout are now prohibited.

Customers also have an expanded right of return. For most online purchases, buyers now have 30 days to return goods for any reason and receive a full refund, up from the previous 14-day window. Items must be returned in their original condition, but retailers cannot charge restocking fees for standard returns.

Business groups have raised concerns that the regulations may increase operating costs, particularly for smaller retailers. Consumer advocacy groups, however, argue that clearer pricing and fairer return policies are long overdue.

questions.q: What do the new regulations require online retailers to do before a purchase is confirmed?
opts: Offer free shipping on all orders
Display all costs including taxes and fees
Provide a written receipt by email
Allow customers to speak with a representative

questions.q: How has the return window for online purchases changed?
opts: It has been reduced from 30 days to 14 days
It has been extended from 14 days to 30 days
It now depends on the type of product
It has been eliminated for digital goods

questions.q: The word 'prohibited' in the first paragraph means:
opts: required
recommended
not allowed
unclear

questions.q: What does the reaction of business groups suggest about the new regulations?
opts: The regulations will likely be reversed within a year
Complying with the regulations may increase costs for some sellers
Most businesses already followed these practices voluntarily
Small businesses will benefit more than large retailers

title: Immigration Settlement Services
text:
The federal government has announced a $45 million funding increase for immigration settlement services over the next three years. The funding will be distributed to community organizations that provide language training, employment assistance, and social integration support to newcomers.

Settlement workers play a key role in helping new immigrants navigate systems such as healthcare, education, and housing. Research shows that newcomers who access settlement services within their first year in Canada are significantly more likely to find stable employment and report higher levels of life satisfaction compared to those who do not.

Demand for settlement services has increased sharply in recent years due to higher immigration targets and the arrival of refugees from several countries. Service providers note that waitlists for language training programs, in particular, have grown to several months in many cities, highlighting the need for additional investment.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the $45 million in new funding?
opts: To build new immigrant processing centres
To support community organizations providing settlement services
To increase the number of immigration officers
To fund language research programs

questions.q: According to the passage, newcomers who access settlement services in their first year are more likely to:
opts: become Canadian citizens faster
find stable employment and report higher life satisfaction
qualify for additional government benefits
be reunited with family members sooner

questions.q: Why have settlement service waitlists grown in recent years?
opts: Services have been cut due to budget constraints
Higher immigration targets and refugee arrivals have increased demand
Many settlement workers have left the profession
The government moved services online

questions.q: Based on the passage, what would most likely happen to a newcomer who does not access settlement services in their first year?
opts: They would be required to reapply for immigration status
They might face greater difficulty finding stable employment
They would lose eligibility for future government support
They would be assigned a settlement worker automatically

title: Electric Vehicle Incentive Program
text:
The provincial government has expanded its electric vehicle incentive program, offering rebates of up to $5,000 for the purchase or lease of a new fully electric vehicle. Plug-in hybrid vehicles are also eligible for a smaller rebate of up to $2,500. The incentive applies to vehicles priced below $55,000 and is available to both individual buyers and businesses.

To qualify, buyers must be residents of the province and must not have previously claimed the rebate. The vehicle must be registered in the province within 30 days of purchase. The rebate is applied at the point of sale, meaning buyers do not need to submit a separate application after the fact.

Provincial officials noted that electric vehicle registrations have grown by 40% year over year. However, access to public charging infrastructure remains uneven, with rural areas receiving fewer investments than urban centres. The government has committed to installing 500 new charging stations across the province by the end of next year.

questions.q: What is the maximum rebate available for a fully electric vehicle?
How is the rebate applied to the purchase?
opts: It is sent by cheque after registration
It is applied at the point of sale
It reduces the annual vehicle tax
It is credited to a future purchase

questions.q: What challenge with electric vehicles does the passage highlight?
opts: The vehicles are too expensive for most buyers
Charging infrastructure is uneven, especially in rural areas
The rebate program has run out of funding

questions.q: Based on the passage, which person would NOT be eligible for the electric vehicle rebate?
opts: A business owner buying a $50,000 fully electric van
A resident purchasing a $54,000 plug-in hybrid
A person who previously claimed the rebate buying a second electric car
A new resident who registered their address in the province two months ago

title: Should Downtown Parking Fees Be Increased?
intro: The city of Millbrook is considering raising parking fees in the downtown core from $3 to $8 per hour to reduce traffic congestion and encourage transit use. Residents have shared a range of views.

comments.name: Karen Osei
comments.text: Higher parking fees are exactly what this city needs. If driving downtown becomes more expensive, people will finally start using the bus and LRT. We can't keep building roads and parking garages forever — it's not sustainable.

comments.name: Derek Fontaine
comments.text: This is just another way to squeeze money out of ordinary people. Not everyone can take transit — I work early mornings and the buses don't run then. Raising fees punishes workers, not wealthy commuters.

comments.name: Priya Mehta
comments.text: I support the idea in principle, but only if the extra revenue is reinvested directly into expanding transit routes and hours. Without that commitment, the policy just creates hardship without fixing the underlying problem.

comments.name: Tom Wiśniewski
comments.text: My concern is the impact on small businesses downtown. When parking gets too expensive, customers shop online or go to suburban malls instead. We've seen that happen in other cities, and it's not good for local merchants.

questions.q: Which person believes that higher parking fees would encourage more people to use public transit?
opts: Derek Fontaine
Karen Osei
Tom Wiśniewski
Priya Mehta

questions.q: Derek Fontaine's main objection to higher parking fees is that:
opts: the fees would be used inefficiently by the city
downtown businesses would lose customers
not all workers have access to transit at the times they need it
the current fee of $3 is already too high

questions.q: What condition does Priya Mehta place on her support for the policy?
opts: The fees must not apply to electric vehicles
The revenue must be used to improve transit services
The increase must be phased in gradually over several years
Businesses must be consulted before the policy takes effect

questions.q: Which person would most likely agree with the statement: "Changes to parking policy could harm the economic vitality of the city centre"?
opts: Karen Osei
Priya Mehta
Derek Fontaine
Tom Wiśniewski

title: Should Landlords Be Required to Allow Pets?
intro: The provincial government is considering legislation that would prevent landlords from including "no-pets" clauses in residential lease agreements. The proposal has prompted strong reactions from renters and property owners alike.

comments.name: Simone Tremblay
comments.text: This legislation is long overdue. Renters with pets are constantly turned away from perfectly good units, and it forces people to choose between their home and their animal companions. Pets are part of the family.

comments.name: Gerald Hawkins
comments.text: As a landlord, I have a right to protect my property. Pet damage — scratched floors, stained carpets, lingering odours — is real and costly. No law should force me to accept that risk in a unit I own.

comments.name: Lucia Ferreira
comments.text: I'd support this if the law also required tenants to carry pet liability insurance and pay a refundable pet deposit. That way landlords have financial protection without being able to ban pets outright.

comments.name: Raj Patel
comments.text: I'm less concerned about the principle than the enforcement. Who decides whether pet damage is beyond normal wear and tear? Without clear standards, this will just lead to more disputes at the Landlord and Tenant Board.

questions.q: Which person most strongly supports the proposed legislation without any conditions?
opts: Gerald Hawkins
Lucia Ferreira
Simone Tremblay
Raj Patel

questions.q: Gerald Hawkins opposes the legislation primarily because:
opts: he believes pets disturb other tenants
he is concerned about the cost of repairing pet-related damage
he thinks landlords already allow pets voluntarily
he disagrees with government involvement in rental agreements

questions.q: What specific safeguard does Lucia Ferreira suggest should accompany the legislation?
opts: A limit on the number of pets per unit
A government fund to cover landlord repair costs
A requirement for pet liability insurance and a refundable deposit
Annual inspections of units where pets are kept

questions.q: Raj Patel's concern suggests he believes the legislation would most likely lead to:
opts: a reduction in available rental housing
increased legal disputes between landlords and tenants
higher rents for all tenants regardless of pet ownership
landlords selling their properties rather than renting them

title: Should Public Libraries Lend Video Games?
intro: Several municipalities across Canada are debating whether public libraries should expand their lending programs to include video games and gaming consoles. The proposal has divided community members.

comments.name: Fatima Al-Rashid
comments.text: Libraries have always evolved with the times — from books to DVDs to e-books. Video games are a legitimate art form and an important part of modern culture. Making them accessible to everyone, regardless of income, is exactly what public libraries are for.

comments.name: Bruce Calloway
comments.text: Libraries are already underfunded. Every dollar spent on gaming consoles and titles is a dollar not spent on books, literacy programs, or community meeting spaces. This is a misuse of public money.

comments.name: Yuki Tanaka
comments.text: I support it, as long as the library focuses on educational and family-friendly titles rather than violent games. There's real value in strategy games, puzzle games, and interactive learning tools for children.

comments.name: Melissa Grant
comments.text: My main question is practical: video games get outdated fast, and consoles need regular replacement. Has anyone actually looked at the long-term maintenance costs? I'd want to see a pilot program with a full cost-benefit analysis before committing.

questions.q: Which person argues that lending video games aligns with the traditional purpose of public libraries?
opts: Bruce Calloway
Fatima Al-Rashid
Melissa Grant
Yuki Tanaka

questions.q: Bruce Calloway's main concern is that:
opts: video games are inappropriate for a public setting
funding games would come at the expense of more essential library services
children would spend too much time gaming instead of reading
the library does not have enough space for gaming equipment

questions.q: What type of video games does Yuki Tanaka consider acceptable for library lending?
opts: Popular titles regardless of content
Only games produced by Canadian developers
Educational and family-friendly games
Games rated for adults only

questions.q: Which person would most likely agree that the library board should gather more data before making a final decision?
opts: Fatima Al-Rashid
Bruce Calloway
Yuki Tanaka
Melissa Grant

title: Understanding Employment Insurance in Canada
text:
Paragraph A
Employment Insurance (EI) is a federal program that provides temporary income support to Canadians who have lost their job through no fault of their own. To be eligible, you must have worked a minimum number of insurable hours in the past 52 weeks. The exact number of hours required depends on the unemployment rate in your region — areas with higher unemployment typically require fewer hours to qualify.

Paragraph B
Applying for EI is done entirely online through the Government of Canada website. You will need your Social Insurance Number, banking information for direct deposit, and details about your employment history, including the name of your last employer and your reason for separation. Most applicants receive a decision within 28 days of submitting their claim.

Paragraph C
Once approved, EI benefits are calculated at 55% of your average insurable weekly earnings, up to a maximum set by the government each year. Payments are made every two weeks. You must continue to meet eligibility requirements throughout the benefit period, which means actively looking for work and reporting any income you earn while receiving benefits.

Paragraph D
Certain life situations allow you to access special EI benefits beyond the standard employment insurance. These include maternity and parental benefits for new parents, sickness benefits for those unable to work due to illness or injury, and compassionate care benefits for people who must take time off to care for a seriously ill family member. Each type has its own eligibility criteria and maximum duration.

questions.q: Which paragraph explains how the amount of EI payments is determined?
opts: Paragraph A
Paragraph B
Paragraph C
Paragraph D

questions.q: According to Paragraph A, what factor affects how many hours a person needs to qualify for EI?
opts: The type of job the person held
The unemployment rate in their region
The number of years they have worked
Whether they quit or were laid off

questions.q: According to Paragraph B, what document is required when applying for EI?
opts: A letter from a previous employer confirming dismissal
A record of all jobs held in the past five years
A Social Insurance Number
Proof of Canadian citizenship

questions.q: The word 'separation' in Paragraph B refers to:
opts: moving to a different city for work
ending an employment relationship
taking an unpaid leave of absence
transferring to another department

title: Types of Rental Arrangements in Canada
text:
Paragraph A
A fixed-term lease is a rental agreement that covers a set period, most commonly 12 months. During this time, neither the landlord nor the tenant can end the agreement early without penalty, unless specific conditions outlined in the lease are met. At the end of the term, the lease either converts to a month-to-month arrangement or is renewed for another fixed period, depending on what both parties agree to.

Paragraph B
A month-to-month tenancy offers more flexibility than a fixed-term lease. Either the landlord or the tenant can end the agreement by giving proper notice — typically 60 days for a tenant and longer for a landlord, depending on provincial rules. Rent can also be reviewed more frequently, though increases must still follow the limits set by provincial legislation.

Paragraph C
Sublet arrangements occur when a tenant temporarily rents their unit to another person while retaining their own lease. The original tenant remains legally responsible for rent and any damage caused by the subtenant. Most leases require the landlord's written permission before subletting, and some landlords may refuse if the lease prohibits it.

Paragraph D
Room rentals, sometimes called boarding arrangements, involve renting a single room within a property where the landlord also lives. These arrangements are often less formal than full-unit leases and may not be covered by the same tenant protection laws. Prospective tenants should clarify what is included — such as kitchen access, utilities, and shared spaces — before agreeing to terms.

questions.q: Which paragraph describes a situation where a tenant lets someone else live in their unit temporarily?
opts: Paragraph A
Paragraph B
Paragraph C
Paragraph D

questions.q: According to Paragraph A, what happens at the end of a fixed-term lease?
opts: The tenant must immediately vacate the unit
The landlord can increase rent by any amount
The lease converts to month-to-month or is renewed
The tenant loses any deposit paid at the start

questions.q: According to Paragraph D, why should tenants in a room rental clarify terms in advance?
opts: Room rentals always cost more than full-unit leases
These arrangements may have fewer legal protections and unclear inclusions
Landlords in room rentals can evict tenants without notice
Room rentals are not permitted in most Canadian provinces

questions.q: The word 'retaining' in Paragraph C is closest in meaning to:
opts: transferring
cancelling
keeping
sharing

title: Waste Sorting in Canadian Municipalities
text:
Paragraph A
Garbage, or residual waste, refers to items that cannot be recycled, composted, or reused. Most municipalities collect garbage on a weekly or biweekly schedule using grey or black bins. Items commonly placed in garbage include broken ceramics, soiled food packaging, and non-recyclable plastics. Many cities have introduced limits on the amount of garbage a household can put out each collection day to encourage waste reduction.

Paragraph B
Recycling programs collect materials that can be processed and turned into new products. Common recyclables include paper, cardboard, glass bottles, metal cans, and many plastic containers. Items must generally be clean and dry before being placed in the recycling bin. Contamination — putting non-recyclable items in the blue bin — is one of the biggest challenges recycling programs face, as it can cause entire loads to be sent to landfill.

Paragraph C
Organics collection, often managed through a green bin program, diverts food waste and yard trimmings from landfills. Accepted materials typically include fruit and vegetable scraps, coffee grounds, eggshells, and grass clippings. Composted organics are processed into nutrient-rich soil amendments used in parks, farms, and gardens. Some municipalities also accept compostable packaging, though this varies by region.

Paragraph D
Hazardous household waste — such as batteries, paint, motor oil, and fluorescent light bulbs — requires special handling because it can be harmful to people and the environment if disposed of incorrectly. Most municipalities operate drop-off depots where residents can bring these items for safe processing. Some communities also hold scheduled collection events throughout the year to make disposal more convenient.

questions.q: Which paragraph explains how food scraps and yard waste are handled?
opts: Paragraph A
Paragraph B
Paragraph C
Paragraph D

questions.q: According to Paragraph B, what is one major problem with recycling programs?
opts: Not enough households participate in recycling
Recycling bins are not large enough for most households
Contamination from non-recyclable items reduces program effectiveness
Recyclable materials take too long to process into new products

questions.q: According to Paragraph D, how can residents dispose of hazardous household waste?
opts: Place it in a clearly labelled garbage bag on collection day
Bring it to a municipal drop-off depot or scheduled collection event
Mix it with regular recycling for special processing
Contact a private waste removal company

questions.q: The word 'diverts' in Paragraph C is closest in meaning to:
opts: collects
redirects away from
increases
breaks down

title: Annual Unit Inspection Notice
text:
Subject: Notice of Annual Unit Inspection — Suite 4B

Dear Resident,

This letter is to inform you that we will be conducting our annual inspection of your unit at 112 Elmwood Avenue on Thursday, October 17th, between 10:00 a.m. and 12:00 p.m. The purpose of this inspection is to check the general condition of the unit and identify any maintenance issues that require attention.

You are not required to be present during the inspection, but you are welcome to stay if you prefer. Please ensure that all areas of the unit, including storage closets and the balcony, are accessible to our maintenance staff.

If the scheduled date is not convenient for you, please contact our office by October 10th to arrange an alternative time. We will make every effort to accommodate your schedule.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,
Darla Hooper
Property Manager, Elmwood Residential

questions.q: What is the main purpose of this letter?
opts: To inform the tenant of a rent increase
To notify the tenant of a scheduled inspection
To report a maintenance problem in the unit
To ask the tenant to renew their lease

questions.q: What must the tenant do if October 17th does not work for them?
opts: Wait for a new notice to be sent
Contact the office before October 10th
Leave a note on their door on the day of the inspection
Call the maintenance staff directly

questions.q: According to the letter, which area of the unit must be made accessible?
opts: Only the kitchen and bathrooms
Only the main living areas
All areas including storage closets and the balcony
Only rooms that have reported problems

questions.q: The letter implies that the tenant:
opts: is required to be home during the inspection
has previously refused an inspection
has the option to be absent when the inspection takes place
must clean the unit before the inspection

title: Road Construction Parking Notice
text:
Subject: Temporary Parking Restrictions — Birchwood Crescent Resurfacing Project

Dear Birchwood Crescent Resident,

The City of Lakeview will be resurfacing Birchwood Crescent from Maple Drive to Cedar Hill Road beginning Monday, November 4th. Work is expected to take approximately ten business days to complete, weather permitting.

During this period, parking on both sides of Birchwood Crescent will be suspended between 7:00 a.m. and 6:00 p.m. on weekdays. Vehicles that remain on the street during restricted hours will be towed at the owner's expense. Overnight parking (6:00 p.m. to 7:00 a.m.) and weekend parking will not be affected.

Residents who do not have access to off-street parking may apply for a temporary permit to use the Lakeview Community Centre parking lot at 88 Riverside Drive, free of charge. Applications must be submitted online at lakeview.ca/permits or in person at City Hall by October 30th.

We apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your patience as we work to improve your neighbourhood.

City of Lakeview Transportation Department

questions.q: What is the main reason for the parking restrictions?
opts: A new housing development is being built nearby
The road on Birchwood Crescent is being resurfaced
A community event is taking place on the street
Emergency utility repairs are being carried out

questions.q: What will happen to vehicles left on the street during restricted hours?
opts: They will receive a parking ticket
They will be towed at the owner's expense
They will be moved to the community centre lot
The owner will be contacted by phone

questions.q: Where can residents without off-street parking apply for a temporary permit?
opts: At the Birchwood Crescent construction office
At the Lakeview Community Centre directly
Online at lakeview.ca/permits or in person at City Hall
By calling the Transportation Department

questions.q: A resident who works Monday to Friday and parks on Birchwood Crescent every night would most likely need to:
opts: find alternative parking only on weekends
apply for a temporary permit or arrange off-street parking on weekdays
avoid the street entirely for ten months
contact the city to request an exemption from the restrictions

title: Employee Benefits Plan Changes
text:
Subject: Important Changes to Your Employee Benefits Plan — Action Required by December 1st

Dear Team Member,

As part of our annual benefits review, Northgate Financial Services will be transitioning to a new benefits provider effective January 1st. Most of your current coverage will remain unchanged; however, there are several updates you should be aware of.

First, the dental coverage maximum will increase from $1,500 to $2,000 per calendar year. Orthodontic coverage, which was previously excluded, will now be available for dependants under the age of 18, up to a lifetime maximum of $3,000 per dependent.

Second, employees who currently use paramedical services — such as physiotherapy, massage therapy, or chiropractic care — will be required to re-register their preferred providers through the new provider's online portal before their first claim in the new year. Claims submitted without prior provider registration will be delayed.

To ensure uninterrupted coverage, all employees must log in to the new benefits portal at northgatebenefits.ca and confirm their personal and dependent information by December 1st. Employees who do not complete this step by the deadline may experience a gap in coverage at the start of the new year.

Please contact HR at benefits@northgatefs.ca if you have any questions.

Human Resources
Northgate Financial Services

questions.q: What is the main purpose of this email?
opts: To announce the company's annual performance review process
To inform employees of upcoming changes to their benefits plan
To introduce a new HR management system
To remind employees to submit their expense claims

questions.q: Under the new plan, which group is newly eligible for orthodontic coverage?
opts: All employees regardless of age
Employees who have been with the company for over five years
Dependants under the age of 18
Retirees and former employees

questions.q: What must employees who use physiotherapy or massage therapy do before making a claim in the new year?
opts: Obtain a referral from their family doctor
Submit receipts from the current year before December 1st
Re-register their providers through the new portal
Switch to providers approved by Northgate Financial Services

questions.q: The phrase "gap in coverage" in the final paragraph most likely means that the employee would:
opts: receive a reduced salary during the transition period
temporarily lose access to their benefits
need to pay higher premiums under the new plan
be required to attend an additional orientation session

title: Clearwater Community Pool — Schedule & Fees
notes: Children under 3 are admitted free when accompanied by a paying adult.
A 10% discount applies to all drop-in fees when purchasing a 10-visit pass.
Aquafit Class is not available to children under 13.

questions.q: What is the drop-in fee for an adult attending the Evening Family Swim?
On which days is the Aquafit Class available?
opts: Every day
Monday, Wednesday, and Friday only
Weekdays only
Saturday and Sunday only

questions.q: A senior who buys a 10-visit pass for Early Bird Lap Swim would pay how much per visit?
Marcus wants to bring his 2-year-old daughter to Saturday morning Family Swim. He will pay:
opts: $6.00 for himself and $3.00 for his daughter
$6.00 for himself only, as his daughter is free
$9.00 total for both
$3.00 for his daughter only

title: City of Harborview — Waste Collection Schedule
notes: When a statutory holiday falls on a collection day, all collections that week shift one day later (e.g., Monday becomes Tuesday).
Bins must be placed at the curb by 7:00 a.m. on collection day. Bins set out after 7:00 a.m. will not be collected until the next scheduled pickup.
Large items must be registered online at harborview.ca/largeitems at least 48 hours before the scheduled pickup date.

questions.q: On which days does Zone B have its Green Bin (organics) collected?
opts: Monday and Thursday
Tuesday and Friday
Wednesday and Saturday
Thursday and Sunday

questions.q: A resident in Zone C wants to put out a large item for pickup. When is their scheduled Large Item Pickup day?
opts: First Monday of the month
First Tuesday of the month
First Wednesday of the month
First Thursday of the month

questions.q: A statutory holiday falls on a Tuesday. A Zone B resident whose garbage is normally collected on Tuesday should expect their collection to occur on:
opts: Monday
Tuesday as usual
Wednesday
The following Tuesday

questions.q: A Zone A resident sets their blue box out at 7:30 a.m. on Monday. Based on the notes, what will most likely happen?
opts: The bin will be collected later the same day
The bin will be collected on the next scheduled pickup
The resident will receive a warning notice
The bin will be collected on Tuesday instead

title: Harborview Municipal Parking — Rate Schedule
notes: Seniors (65+) and persons with a valid disability permit receive a 50% discount on hourly rates only. Daily maximums, evening flat rates, and monthly passes are not discounted.
Monthly passes are not available for Lot E (Hospital). Hospital visitors may validate their parking ticket at the main reception desk for a $2.00 reduction on stays under 2 hours.
Overnight parking (10:00 p.m.–6:00 a.m.) is prohibited in all lots except Lot D.

questions.q: What is the daily maximum charge at the Central Library parking lot (Lot B)?
A senior parks at Lot A for 3 hours during the day. How much will they pay?
Which lot is the only one that permits overnight parking?
opts: Lot A
Lot C
Lot D
Lot E

questions.q: A hospital visitor parks in Lot E for 90 minutes and validates their ticket at reception. How much will they pay?
